headlines.title: Global stocks soar and oil prices drop as U.S., Iran reach tentative deal to end war
TSX hits new record high as oil prices slide after U.S.-Iran deal
Stocks leap worldwide, and oil prices drop after the US and Iran reach a tentative deal on their war
Oil prices drop to cheapest level since early days of Middle East conflict
Hopes for an Extended Ceasefire Lift Stocks

notes: Equities rallied and oil prices fell significantly following the announcement of a tentative peace deal and extended ceasefire between the US and Iran.
The tentative agreement includes the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, easing global inflation and energy supply fears.
S&P 500 closed up roughly 1.65% at 7554.29, while the NASDAQ surged over 3% as risk-on sentiment returned to the markets.
Brent Crude dropped nearly 5% to the low $83 range on the prospect of resumed crude flows.
VIX fell over 5% as market volatility expectations cooled on the geopolitical breakthrough.
